_description: Maps Princeton WordNet 'lexicographer file' codes to Linguics theme IDs (from vocab_themes.json). Used by Code's bulk-translation/bulk-theming pipeline (see BRIEF_for_code_bulk_translations.md). When a wordnet lookup returns multiple lex files for a lemma, union the theme arrays. Empty array means 'no useful Linguics theme; fall back to POS-default'.
_source: Princeton WordNet 3.0 lexicographer file naming; mappings authored by vocab chat 2026-05-27
_notes: Mappings are conservative: when a wordnet category is too broad to map to a specific Linguics theme (e.g. noun.artifact, noun.group, noun.act, noun.object), an empty array is returned so the POS-default placeholder kicks in.

Some categories map to multiple Linguics themes in principle (e.g. noun.body might also indicate health_medicine for some lemmas like organo, polmone). Code does not attempt this disambiguation; vocab chat handles it in refinement passes.

Verb categories: WordNet's verb.body covers grooming/hygiene verbs which map to verb_routine. verb.competition is mostly verb_action_general. verb.contact (touch, hit) is verb_action_general — vocab chat may later promote some to verb_destruction.
Sub-theme assignment (e.g. food_fruit, animals_pet) is NOT derivable from lexicographer files. Use data/lemma_subthemes.json for those.
